text:
Market today: Wednesday rotation is the theme (Cramer headline confirms it). Tech/chips are seeing money shuffle around - not a breakdown, but not a clean bid either. CoreWeave/Nebius tumbling on Meta cloud threat signals the AI infrastructure trade is getting crowded and competitive, which is the real driver for your book today.

Your holdings: SOXL (-14.52%) is the one screaming for attention - 3x leveraged, already deep in the hole, stop at $200 with price at $217.55. That gap is thin and decay is eating you alive. SPAL (-13.72%) is brand-new, very volatile, leveraged ETF with a stop at $21.38 vs. $21.70 price - basically already at the door. SMH (-2.5%) is the quieter worry; if chips stay soft, this drags further and squeezes your margin cushion. Note: margin amplifies every one of these drops.

Watch: SOXL holding above $200 stop - that level breaks and you're forced out ugly. Also watch SMH for follow-through weakness given the Meta/cloud AI sentiment hit.

Bottom line: Your leveraged losers are the fire to put out before anything else.
plays: SOXL: Stop at $200 is your line - if price approaches, tighten or exit now; decay plus margin makes holding a deep leveraged loser very costly, no reason to wait for the stop to get hit.
SPAL: At $21.70 with stop $21.38, you're 1.5% from forced exit on a leveraged ETF already down 13.7% - consider cutting now rather than letting a fast-moving name stop you out on a gap.
No new buys today - hold and watch; scanner setups exist but no headline catalyst strong enough to justify adding fresh margin exposure while your leveraged names are under pressure.
